action.reasoning: Observation: Updated predict.py with robust custom get_spherical_cameras_with_azimuth (eps=1e-8 normalize prevents NaNs, device/tensor handling) completed successfully on full val set (~175s, all objects rendered with per-target rel_az=(target_az-input_az)%360 + exact elev/dist, RGBA outputs, no crashes). Prior LPIPS=0.6121 from default orbit (fixed az~0 start). Analysis: Pose mismatch was root cause of poor score (dataset target az vary widely ~100-300deg rel to input; custom rays match inspected TripoSR get_spherical_cameras + renderer logic exactly). Zero-shot TripoSR + per-view alignment is task-optimal (<1B params, no training needed, fits all constraints). Val test confirms predict.py meets eval contract (100 objs, correct dir structure, 256x256 RGBA). Decision: Submit eval immediately on current best.pt + predict.py for new LPIPS (expect major drop) + time measurement per workflow. No code changes or training yet.
action.command: echo 'Test completed successfully, submitting eval now'
